# Site Inventory — Apex Roofing Co.

**Run:** 20260509T1200-roofing  
**Industry Pack:** local-services → roofing  
**Visual Archetype:** local-business-trust (elevated)  
**Quality Target:** world_class

---

## Tier 1 — Infrastructure (auto-generated, no brief)

These routes are generated by `frontend_developer` from standard patterns. No per-page design brief is required or emitted.

| Route | File | Conditional? | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `/not-found` | `app/not-found.tsx` | No | 404 with home link and brand colors |
| `/error` | `app/error.tsx` | No | Runtime error boundary with retry + home link |
| `loading` | `app/loading.tsx` (per route group) | No | Skeleton fallback per route group |
| `/privacy-policy` | `app/(legal)/privacy-policy/page.tsx` | No | Standard legal layout; content from legal locale file, not main content library |
| `/terms-of-service` | `app/(legal)/terms-of-service/page.tsx` | No | Standard legal layout; same locale file |
| `sitemap.ts` | `app/sitemap.ts` | No | Auto-generated from route inventory |
| `robots.ts` | `app/robots.ts` | No | Standard allow all; disallow admin |
| `manifest.ts` | `app/manifest.ts` | No | PWA manifest with brand colors |
| OG image | `app/opengraph-image.tsx` | No | Brand token default (slate-navy bg, amber-copper accent) |
| `icon.tsx` | `app/icon.tsx` | No | Placeholder; real asset supplied by human |
| `apple-icon.tsx` | `app/apple-icon.tsx` | No | Placeholder; real asset supplied by human |
| `/cookies` | — | **Not required** | UK/EU GDPR scope not declared in brief; US residential market only |
| `/accessibility-statement` | — | **Not required** | Public sector compliance not declared in brief |

> **NOTE on Privacy & Terms:** The run folder contains `pages/privacy.md` and `pages/terms.md` as Tier 2 briefs authored during the planning session. These are **superseded** by Tier 1 auto-generation. The developer MUST use `app/(legal)/privacy-policy/page.tsx` and `app/(legal)/terms-of-service/page.tsx` as Tier 1 routes. The Tier 2 brief files (`pages/privacy.md`, `pages/terms.md`) are retained as content reference only — they supply the content slot list; developer derives layout from Tier 1 standard template.

---

## Tier 2 — Core Business Routes (always briefed)

Intent mapping for `local_services` archetype: flagship_landing, service_capability, trust_credibility, direct_conversion.

| Intent | Route | Brief file | Creative latitude |
|---|---|---|---|
| `flagship_landing` | `/` | `pages/home.md` | HIGH |
| `service_capability` | `/services` | `pages/services.md` | HIGH |
| `trust_credibility` | `/reviews` | `pages/reviews.md` | MEDIUM |
| `trust_credibility` | `/about` | `pages/about.md` | MEDIUM |
| `direct_conversion` | `/quote` | `pages/quote.md` | LOW |
| `direct_conversion` | `/contact` | `pages/contact.md` | LOW |

---

## Tier 3 — Business-Specific Routes

Identified from brief signals: residential/commercial roofing market, emergency response selling point, geographic service area coverage, distinct service lines, and blog/content marketing intent.

| Route | Brief file | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| `/services/roof-installation` | `pages/services-roof-installation.md` | Distinct service line requiring its own trust proof, pricing signals, and conversion path; brief identifies installation as a major revenue stream. |
| `/services/roof-repair` | `pages/services-roof-repair.md` | High-volume residential intent; distinct urgency + damage-assessment narrative vs. installation. |
| `/services/roof-replacement` | `pages/services-roof-replacement.md` | High-value job category; requires financing messaging, material comparison, and warranty emphasis not needed on repair page. |
| `/services/emergency-repair` | `pages/services-emergency-repair.md` | 24/7 emergency response is a primary differentiator in the roofing market; requires urgency UI patterns not present on standard service pages. |
| `/areas` | `pages/areas.md` | Local SEO critical — each coverage city requires a named mention; drives geo-targeted organic traffic. |
| `/faq` | `pages/faq.md` | Roofing purchases have high information cost; FAQ reduces pre-sale phone volume and supports SEO long-tail. |
| `/blog` | `pages/blog.md` | Content marketing for seasonal roofing topics; supports organic acquisition and establishes authority. |

---

## Brief Scope

The `pages/` brief folder contains briefs for **Tier 2 + Tier 3** routes only.

| Brief file | Tier | Route |
|---|---|---|
| `pages/home.md` | Tier 2 | `/` |
| `pages/services.md` | Tier 2 | `/services` |
| `pages/reviews.md` | Tier 2 | `/reviews` |
| `pages/about.md` | Tier 2 | `/about` |
| `pages/quote.md` | Tier 2 | `/quote` |
| `pages/contact.md` | Tier 2 | `/contact` |
| `pages/services-roof-installation.md` | Tier 3 | `/services/roof-installation` |
| `pages/services-roof-repair.md` | Tier 3 | `/services/roof-repair` |
| `pages/services-roof-replacement.md` | Tier 3 | `/services/roof-replacement` |
| `pages/services-emergency-repair.md` | Tier 3 | `/services/emergency-repair` |
| `pages/areas.md` | Tier 3 | `/areas` |
| `pages/faq.md` | Tier 3 | `/faq` |
| `pages/blog.md` | Tier 3 | `/blog` |
| `pages/not-found.md` | Tier 2* | `/not-found` |
| `pages/privacy.md` | Reference only | *(superseded by Tier 1)* |
| `pages/terms.md` | Reference only | *(superseded by Tier 1)* |

*`not-found.md` brief was authored and is provided as design guidance for the 404 surface; developer may enrich the Tier 1 auto-template using its content references.

## Auth Routes

**Not required.** This project is a marketing-only site for a roofing contractor. No user accounts, sign-in, or sign-up surfaces are planned.

Consequence: `AuthModal`, `AuthModalProvider`, `/sign-in`, `/sign-up` are **explicitly opted out**.

```yaml
auth_modal_opt_out: true
opt_out_justification: "Marketing-only site. No authentication surface required. No user accounts."
```

Developer MUST NOT generate `AuthModal`, `AuthModalProvider`, or auth route stubs. INV3 check is `not-applicable` for this project.

---

## Execution Notes for `frontend_developer`

1. `execution_mode: frontend_focus` — OpenAPI spec is absent; generate typed mock adapters for any CMS/form surfaces.
2. Sanity CMS is declared in the tech stack; CMS-backed pages (blog, reviews) use mock adapter in dev, real Sanity client in production build. This project is classified as `baseline_prototype` until Sanity is wired.
3. Root package.json shim: generate a root-level `package.json` at `web/../package.json` (repo root) with script proxies to `web/`. This file lives **outside** `web/` but is a developer ergonomic file, not a frontend artifact — it is exempt from the "all files under `web/`" rule.
4. All Tier 1 routes must be generated even without a brief.
